description: To 'therapi' is to heat a liquid, or food in liquid, until bubbles form and rise continuously through it. The verb also fits the liquid reaching that state without a named outside agent. 'pharelo' (cook) covers heat preparation broadly; boiling selects the moving liquid condition.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and release the breath into true-mid front 'e'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip before stressed central 'a'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into final high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: Soft dental breath enters the broad tongue movement of stressed 'ra'. Final 'pi' closes the lips and breaks into a bright small vowel. The centre swells before the finish breaks cleanly, an embodied echo of bubbles reaching a surface.
usage_notes: The verb can be transitive when someone boils a liquid or intransitive when the liquid boils. A vessel and heat source take their ordinary phrases. As a noun, the same form names boiling; exact process conditions remain with the source instruction or record.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The sound and movement of a pot told a cook when water had reached a boil long before a numbered instrument stood beside the hearth.

semantic_domains.physical: a liquid condition with bubbles rising continuously
